prompt_inputs.goal: Marathon training with carbohydrate loading for high-endurance performance

solution_criteria: Meal plan contains 4-5 meals/snacks distributed throughout the day with timing around training
Prioritizes energy-dense, carbohydrate-rich foods (pasta, rice, oats, bread) comprising at least 60% of calories
Includes protein sources for muscle recovery and hydration guidance
Estimated daily caloric intake aligns with marathon runner requirements (3000-3500 calories)
test_case.task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete.
test_case.scenario: Testing with a high-endurance athlete (marathon runner) requiring high calorie intake and carbohydrate loading, ensuring the meal plan prioritizes energy-dense foods and timing around training

reasoning: The solution comprehensively satisfies all mandatory requirements with a well-organized, detailed meal plan. It includes exact daily caloric intake (3,500 kcal), complete macronutrient breakdown with targets and actuals, and 6 meals/snacks with precise portions and timing. The carbohydrate loading strategy (60% of calories) is appropriate for marathon training. All secondary criteria are met: 6 meals distributed throughout the day, energy-dense carb-rich foods emphasized, adequate protein included, and caloric intake within the 3,000-3,500 range. Minor weaknesses exist in macro calculation precision and training session context, but these do not violate mandatory requirements. The solution is professional, practical, and directly addresses the athlete's marathon training goal.

prompt_inputs.goal: Muscle recovery and strength gains from weightlifting training

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers all meals for one full day
Protein is distributed across at least 4 meals/snacks with minimum 30g per serving
Total daily protein meets or exceeds 1.6g per kg of body weight (136g)
Includes post-workout recovery nutrition with carbohydrates and protein
test_case.task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete.
test_case.scenario: Testing with a strength-based athlete (weightlifter) with specific protein requirements and muscle recovery needs, verifying the plan includes adequate protein distribution across meals and recovery nutrition

reasoning: The solution fully satisfies all mandatory requirements: it includes daily caloric total (3,200 kcal), complete macronutrient breakdown (170g protein, 360g carbs, 85g fat), meals with exact foods, portions, and timing. All secondary criteria are met: covers all meals for one full day (6 meals/snacks), protein distributed across 6 meals with minimum 30g per serving, exceeds the 1.6g/kg protein requirement (170g vs 136g minimum), and includes post-workout recovery nutrition (shake at 3:30 PM with 35g protein and 60g carbs). The meal plan is well-organized, practical, and includes hydration guidance. The only minor issue is that the protein target appears unnecessarily elevated beyond the stated criterion, though this doesn't violate any requirements—it simply exceeds them.

prompt_inputs.goal: Early morning swim training (5:00 AM start) with energy for 90-minute high-intensity session

solution_criteria: Includes a pre-dawn meal (before 5:00 AM) that is easily digestible and won't cause stomach discomfort during swimming
Provides adequate hydration strategy throughout the day with specific fluid recommendations
All meals and snacks comply with stated dietary restrictions
Plan is compact and practical for a single day with realistic preparation time constraints
test_case.task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete.
test_case.scenario: Testing with a sport-specific constraint scenario (swimmer with early morning training) requiring pre-dawn meal options and hydration focus, ensuring practicality for unusual training schedules

reasoning: The solution comprehensively meets all mandatory requirements with precise daily totals, macronutrient targets, and detailed meal specifications with timing. It excellently addresses the secondary criteria: the pre-dawn meal is appropriately light and easily digestible, hydration is well-planned, all restrictions are respected, and the plan is compact and practical. The minor weaknesses around snack sizing and electrolyte drink specificity do not undermine the core quality of the plan. The solution is well-structured, realistic, and directly addresses the athlete's early morning training needs.
